Politics.headline: Gulf states press U.S. to neutralize Iran for good as Hormuz crisis deepens
Politics.detail: Persian Gulf states are urging the U.S. not to stop short of comprehensively degrading Iran's military capacity, as the war enters its third week with intensified U.S. and Israeli airstrikes and Iranian responses across the region. They fear that unless Iran is severely weakened, it will continue to pose a constant threat to the region.

Politics.headline: Egypt's president condemns Iranian attacks, backs Saudi Arabia
Politics.detail: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi condemned Iranian attacks on Saudi Arabia and other Arab countries, calling them violations of sovereignty. He reaffirmed Egypt's support for the kingdom and stressed the importance of de-escalation, highlighting close security ties between Egypt and Gulf countries.

Politics.headline: Iran negotiating with FIFA to move World Cup 2026 matches from US to Mexico
Politics.detail: Iran's football federation is in discussions with FIFA to potentially relocate its World Cup 2026 matches from the United States to Mexico, citing concerns about player safety amidst the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran. Iranian football president Mehdi Taj stated that the team would not travel to America if President Trump could not ensure their security.

Politics.headline: Will the Iran war turn midwest swing states against Trump after his 'America first' promise?
Politics.detail: Trump's perceived failure to keep his word regarding his 'America First' promise could be significant in the upcoming primaries and midterm elections, particularly for independent voters in Midwest swing states. The ongoing war with Iran raises questions about the potential impact on these states.

Politics.headline: Our generation is planting the seeds of system failure in international law, but it is our children who will have to harvest this violent future.
Politics.detail: The International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) warns that the current generation is setting the stage for future failures in international law, leading to a violent future for children. They highlight that global military spending is at record highs and that an increasing number of children are living in conflict-affected areas.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gulf banks face $307 billion deposit flight risk if war drags on, S&P says
Finance & Economy.detail: Gulf banks have so far avoided significant deposit outflows but face domestic funding drain of up to $307 billion if the Iran war continues, according to S&P.

Finance & Economy.headline: Japan and US to jointly develop rare earths, lithium, copper
Finance & Economy.detail: Japan and the United States are set to agree to jointly develop rare earths, lithium and copper.

Finance & Economy.headline: Germany aims to double data centre capacity by 2030
Finance & Economy.detail: Germany plans to at least double its domestic data centre capacity and boost artificial intelligence data.

Finance & Economy.headline: New mortgages up by £800 a year amid 'Trumpflation' from Iran war
Finance & Economy.detail: New mortgages in the UK are up by £800 a year amid 'Trumpflation' resulting from the Iran war.

Finance & Economy.headline: Cruise lines face fuel cost surge as oil prices jump on Iran tensions
Finance & Economy.detail: Cruise lines face rising fuel costs as oil prices surge 35% during the Iran conflict, threatening profits during peak booking season for summer trips.

Technology.headline: Gartner Predicts AI Applications Will Drive 50% of Cybersecurity Incident Response Efforts by 2028
Technology.detail: According to Gartner, Inc., fifty percent of all enterprise cybersecurity incident response efforts will focus on incidents involving custom-built AI-driven applications by 2028, as these systems are complex, dynamic and difficult to secure over time. Most security teams still lack clear processes for handling AI-related incidents, which means issues can take longer to resolve and require far more effort.

Technology.headline: RTX's Collins Aerospace announces completion of Clean Aviation Joint Undertaking HECATE project
Technology.detail: Collins Aerospace, an RTX business, announced the successful completion of the HECATE project, part of the European Union's Clean Aviation Joint Undertaking with support from UK Research and Innovation. This initiative achieved Technology Readiness Level 5, successfully proving the electrical architecture design for future hybrid-electric aircraft performance in real-world conditions.

Technology.headline: Successful Flight Demonstration of Mission Autonomy Developed for Use in Unmanned Aerial Vehicles-- Leveraging an AI Development Environment through Hivemind Enterprise in Collaboration with US Company Shield AI --
Technology.detail: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, Ltd. (MHI) has successfully conducted flight demonstrations of AI-powered mission autonomy developed for use in unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) by leveraging an AI development environment through Hivemind Enterprise provided by US company Shield AI Inc. The entire process, from AI development to UAV installation and flight, was completed in just eight weeks.

Technology.headline: UK must learn lessons from AI race and retain its quantum computing talent, says minister
Technology.detail: Liz Kendall announces £1bn funding to help design large-scale quantum computers for scientists, researchers, public sector and business.

Technology.headline: Children being 'failed by tech companies' amid rise in online sex abuse images
Technology.detail: The NSPCC said that "device-level protections" should be embedded by default to ensure children are granted automatic protection, while adult users would have the choice to opt out. The technology can block a nude image from being taken, sent, or received on a device, which is a system the NSPCC said can enable abuse to be stopped at the source and prevent indecent images from ever being created or sent.

Health.headline: Meningitis in fatal Kent outbreak identified as less-targeted strain B
Health.detail: The meningitis outbreak at the University of Kent, which has resulted in two deaths, has been identified as a less-targeted strain B, raising concerns about the effectiveness of current vaccination efforts.

Health.headline: Officials 'missed 99% of data' before ending Covid vaccine recommendation, memos reveal
Health.detail: Memos reveal that officials missed a significant amount of data before deciding to end the recommendation for COVID-19 vaccines, raising questions about the decision-making process.

Health.headline: Just 24 Minutes of Specially Designed Music Could Significantly Reduce Anxiety
Health.detail: A clinical trial showed that listening to music specifically designed with auditory beat stimulation for just 24 minutes can lead to a significant reduction in anxiety levels.

Health.headline: Antithrombotic Choice May Affect Bleed Risk in Endocarditis
Health.detail: The selection of antithrombotic medication could influence the risk of bleeding complications in patients suffering from endocarditis, according to a recent report.

Health.headline: Cuban Doctors Keep Calabria's Hospitals Running
Health.detail: A pandemic-era rule allows Calabria to temporarily accept foreign medical degrees, enabling Cuban doctors to staff critical anesthesia, cardiology, and emergency positions.

Sports.headline: NBA: Red-hot Southeast with Hawks, Magic, Heat and Hornets surging
Sports.detail: The Southeast Division is making waves in the NBA with all four teams, the Hawks, Magic, Heat, and Hornets, showing strong performances.

Sports.headline: Mexico City sets record for largest soccer class
Sports.detail: Mexico City has achieved a new record for hosting the largest soccer class, promoting the sport at the grassroots level.

Sports.headline: Kerr and Foord propel improved Matildas past China to final
Sports.detail: Kerr and Foord led the Matildas to a strong victory against China, securing their place in the Women's Asian Cup final.

Sports.headline: Laporta wins 5 more years as president of Barcelona after thousands vote in club election
Sports.detail: Joan Laporta has been re-elected as the president of Barcelona for another 5 years following a club election with thousands of voters.

Sports.headline: Sam Farmer's NFL mock draft 1.0: Tom Brady's Raiders won't pass on a quarterback.
Sports.detail: In his initial NFL mock draft, Sam Farmer projects that Tom Brady's Raiders will prioritize selecting a quarterback.

Entertainment.headline: Salman Khan, Chitrangda Singh light up 'Chand Dekh Lena' song teaser from Maatrubhumi
Entertainment.detail: The teaser of 'Chand Dekh Lena' from the movie *Maatrubhumi* featuring Salman Khan and Chitrangada Singh, has been released, showcasing the on-screen chemistry between the actors. The song is about an army officer and his wife, and how their relationship survives the challenges of war.

Entertainment.headline: How Canadian film 'Undertone' became a box-office hit despite public funding rejection
Entertainment.detail: The Canadian horror film *Undertone*, directed by Ian Tuason, achieved unexpected box office success with a $9.3 million debut, despite being rejected by Canadian public film funders. Tuason critiqued Canada's film funding system, suggesting a need to re-evaluate funding allocation based on viewership and demand.

Entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 gets 24-hr shows due to huge demand; pushes DDLJ at Maratha Mandir
Entertainment.detail: Due to the high demand for *Dhurandhar 2*, the film is getting 24-hour shows, which is impacting the screenings of *DDLJ* at Maratha Mandir. Ranveer Singh stars as an Indian spy in Karachi in *Dhurandhar 2*.

Entertainment.headline: Sean Penn skipped Oscars to take train to war-torn Ukraine, met Zelenskyy
Entertainment.detail: Sean Penn, who won an Oscar for Best Supporting Actor, skipped the awards ceremony to visit Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Kyiv. Penn's visit highlights his ongoing support for Ukraine amidst the ongoing war.

Entertainment.headline: Salim Khan to be discharged from hospital one month after brain haemorrhage
Entertainment.detail: Veteran screenwriter Salim Khan, father of Salman Khan, is being discharged from the hospital a month after suffering a brain haemorrhage. Salim Khan's discharge brings relief to his family and fans.

Crime & Justice.headline: Children to get swifter justice as new family court approach expands nationally
Crime & Justice.detail: Child Focused Courts, which have proven to resolve cases quicker, will be rolled out nationwide in England and Wales to protect children from trauma and deliver fairer, faster justice; the government will also remove parental responsibility from people convicted of serious sexual offenses against children.

Crime & Justice.headline: Owners and CEO of Wholesale Pharmaceutical Company Sentenced for Distributing More Than $92M of Black-Market HIV Drugs
Crime & Justice.detail: The owners and CEO of a wholesale pharmaceutical company have been sentenced for distributing over $92 million of black-market HIV drugs.

Crime & Justice.headline: CPS announces man charged with the murder of Ian Huntley at HMP Frankland.
Crime & Justice.detail: Anthony Russell, 43, has been charged with the murder of Ian Huntley at HMP Frankland.

Crime & Justice.headline: High-Level Side Events on the Sidelines of the 70th Session of the Commission on the Status of Women (CSW70)
Crime & Justice.detail: The African Union Department of Health, Humanitarian Affairs and Social Development (HHS) convened two high-level side events focusing on strengthening legal and community justice systems to protect girls against cross-border trafficking and building technical capacity for domestic financing of the AU Accountability Framework on Ending Harmful Practices.

Crime & Justice.headline: Baroness Kidron's amendment to the Crime and Policing Bill on AI chatbots will be debated in the House of Lords on 18 March.
Crime & Justice.detail: Baroness Kidron is proposing to tighten online safety laws around AI chatbots – and to crack down on the people and companies that deploy them irresponsibly; if passed, Kidron's amendment to the Crime and Policing Bill would make it a criminal offence to create a chatbot that produces content that is illegal, or harmful, exploitative or coercive towards children.

Education.headline: Heatwave In Surat: Schools Suspend Outdoor Activities, Enforces Safety Measures
Education.detail: Due to the ongoing heatwave in Surat, schools have suspended outdoor activities and are enforcing safety measures to protect students. The specific measures being taken were not detailed in the summary.

Education.headline: Eid-ul-Fitr 2026: Will Schools Be Closed On This Date? Here's What We Know
Education.detail: As Eid-ul-Fitr approaches, many are wondering whether schools will be closed on this date. NDTV Education provides information on school closures related to the holiday.

Education.headline: PM-Vidyalaxmi Completes One Year With 50 Per Cent Loan Approval Rate On Portal, Rs 36,000 Crore Sanctioned
Education.detail: The PM-Vidyalaxmi scheme, a program for higher education loans, has completed one year, achieving a 50 percent loan approval rate through its portal and sanctioning Rs 36,000 crore. This milestone indicates the program's progress in facilitating access to higher education financing.

Education.headline: SSC 2026 Revised Exam Calendar Out: Check Dates For Combined Graduate Level, CHSL And Other Exams
Education.detail: The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the revised exam calendar for 2026, which includes dates for the Combined Graduate Level (CGL), Combined Higher Secondary Level (CHSL), and other exams. Candidates can check the official notification for the updated exam schedule and plan their preparation accordingly.

Education.headline: CTET Feb 2026 Exam: Check Expected Result Date, Passing Marks, Scorecard Details
Education.detail: For those who took the CTET (Central Teacher Eligibility Test) in February 2026, information is available regarding the expected result date, passing marks, and scorecard details. Candidates can refer to updates for the latest information on their CTET results.

Lifestyle.headline: Oscars 2026: Gwyneth Paltrow's High-Slit Armani Prive Gown Sparks Buzz Online
Lifestyle.detail: Gwyneth Paltrow's choice of a high-slit Armani Prive gown at the 2026 Oscars has generated significant buzz and discussion online.

Lifestyle.headline: Inside Chanel and Charles Finch's glamorous pre-Oscars dinner at the Polo Lounge
Lifestyle.detail: A glamorous pre-Oscars dinner hosted by Chanel and Charles Finch took place at the Polo Lounge, drawing notable attendees.

Lifestyle.headline: Woman's alarming cancer symptoms blamed on pregnancy for years before Stage 3 diagnosis
Lifestyle.detail: A woman experienced alarming cancer symptoms that were initially misattributed to pregnancy for several years before receiving a Stage 3 diagnosis.

Lifestyle.headline: Dr Madhu Chopra drops a glimpse of injecting herself with Botox on right side of face, says doesn't go overboard: 'I feel it's drooping a little bit'
Lifestyle.detail: Dr. Madhu Chopra shared a glimpse of herself receiving a Botox injection on the right side of her face, explaining that she prefers a subtle approach because she felt a slight drooping.

Lifestyle.headline: Women reveal surprising effects of being bald: 'It's a luxury'
Lifestyle.detail: Women are sharing the unexpected benefits they've experienced from being bald.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: How to solve the Hormuz crisis
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post's editorial board discusses potential solutions to the Hormuz crisis, suggesting that air supremacy doesn't equate to sea control and proposing declaring victory and withdrawing.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Trump's war with Iran: if the US is winning, why ask Nato for help?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Guardian's editorial board questions the rationale behind seeking NATO assistance if the US is supposedly winning the war with Iran.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Young people want to work: now there may be jobs for them
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Polly Toynbee writes about spending a week at a London jobcentre and observing that many smart and eager people are looking for work, and now there's a government willing to help them.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: No, MAGA is not divided on the Iran war
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece on Al Jazeera discusses the unified stance of the MAGA movement regarding the Iran war.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Soldiers are dying in a war with Iran, and Ted Cruz jokes on Twitter | Opinion
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece in the Houston Chronicle comments on Ted Cruz making jokes on Twitter as soldiers are dying in the war with Iran.

World.headline: Israel says it has killed Iran's security chief Ali Larijani
World.detail: Israel claims it killed Ali Larijani, the secretary of Iran's supreme national security council, in overnight strikes. The attack is part of the ongoing conflict between the two nations.

World.headline: Islandwide blackout hits Cuba as it struggles with deepening energy crisis
World.detail: Cuba is experiencing a complete electrical grid collapse amid a worsening energy crisis. The blackout compounds the difficulties faced by residents already struggling with shortages.

World.headline: Trump-Xi summit delayed as U.S. president pushes China to help open Hormuz
World.detail: The meeting between the U.S. and Chinese leaders has been postponed as Trump pressures China to assist in reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The delay occurs amidst rising tensions and efforts to resolve the oil route's closure.

World.headline: UK to roll out red carpet for Nigeria in first state visit since 1989.
World.detail: The United Kingdom is preparing to host Nigeria for a state visit, marking the first such event between the two nations since 1989. This visit signifies a strengthening of diplomatic and economic ties.

World.headline: Gulf economies suffer brunt of Iran war as recession risk looms
World.detail: The ongoing conflict with Iran is negatively impacting the economies of Gulf nations, increasing the risk of recession in the region. The war is disrupting trade and stability, leading to economic concerns.

Zimbabwe.headline: Harare City Council to slash business licence fees by 50pc
Zimbabwe.detail: Harare City Council is planning to reduce business license fees by 50 percent in an effort to create a more business-friendly environment and encourage economic activity within the city.

Zimbabwe.headline: Afreximbank appoints Zimbabwean Lesley Ndlovu to lead AfrexInsure scale-up
Zimbabwe.detail: Afreximbank has appointed Zimbabwean Lesley Ndlovu to lead the scale-up of AfrexInsure, the bank's insurance arm.

Zimbabwe.headline: Residents protest $580 'illegal settler' penalty in Umzingwane
Zimbabwe.detail: Residents in Umzingwane are protesting a $580 penalty imposed on those deemed 'illegal settlers' in the area.

Zimbabwe.headline: Foreign Investor Duped Of US$1.5 Million In Mining Deal
Zimbabwe.detail: A foreign investor has allegedly been duped of US$1.5 million in a mining deal in Zimbabwe, highlighting risks in the sector.

Zimbabwe.headline: RBZ seeks business buy-in for new ZiG notes
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) is engaging with businesses to encourage acceptance and adoption of the new ZiG notes.
